title: M-class
content:
{{Top|leg|title=''M''-class}}
{{Otheruses|M-class}}
[[File:Lady_Mindor.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The ''Lady of Mindor'', an ''M''-class luxury liner]]
'''M-class''' was a category of [[Starship/Legends|starship]] defined in the ''[[Interstellar Starshipwright's Guild Book of Specifications and Classifications]]''. The designation was commonly assigned to [[Passenger liner/Legends|passenger]] and [[Luxury liner/Legends|luxury liners]], as well as military grade [[Pocket cruiser/Legends|pocket cruisers]].<ref name="GSWU">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]''</ref>

==History==
Among ships known to have been designated as ''M''-class were ''[[Lady of Mindor]]'',<ref name="HSR">''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]''</ref> a 310-[[Meter/Legends|meter]], 600-passenger [[Lady-class Luxury Liner|''Lady''-class]] ship from [[Shobquix Yards]],<ref name="HSCS">''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]''</ref> and also the 500-meter-long [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] vessel [[Kuari Princess/Legends|''Kuari Princess'']], which carried 3,500 tourists.<ref name="RPG1">[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The ''M''-class designation is first used to describe the ''Lady of Mindor'' in ''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]''. The ''Kuari Princess'' was described as an '' 'M-class luxury liner' '' in [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']].

==Appearances==
*''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]''
